commit.message:
Fix npm detection: use exit code instead of output string

The previous logic checked if "npm" appeared in output, but error messages
like "which: npm: Command not found" also contain "npm", causing false
positives. Now uses the exit code from `which` directly.

commit.message:
Fix game hang by correcting CPU branch timing

Branch taken (+1) and page-crossing (+2) cycle penalties were lost because
branch() incremented this.cycles but execute() overwrote it with the base
cost. Use a branchPenalty field that gets added after execute() returns.
Also remove temporary debug instrumentation from PPU and NesEmulator.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Remove unused network API dependencies

- Remove -lbnetapi from Makefile
- Update README.md links
- Remove net API docs from memory
- The program doesn't use any network functionality
- Keep -ltracker for BFilePanel

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: HaiClaude
repository.description: Native Haiku GUI launcher for Claude Code

commit.message:
Add component diagram in draw.io format

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix game hang after screensaver by delivering APU frame counter IRQs to CPU

The APU's isIrqPending() was never checked in the main loop, so games
relying on APU IRQs for timing (e.g. Double Dragon & Battletoads) would
spin indefinitely waiting for an interrupt that never fired.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add APU implementation for sound support

Implement NES Audio Processing Unit with pulse, triangle, and noise
channels, wired into the CPU bus and main emulation loop. Audio output
uses javax.sound.sampled at 44100 Hz.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix game freeze after title screen by adding NMI edge detection, PRG RAM, and DMA NMI handling

- Trigger NMI immediately when game enables NMI via PPUCTRL while VBlank is active
- Add 8KB PRG RAM at $6000-$7FFF so game state reads/writes work correctly
- Check for NMI during DMA transfer to avoid missing VBlank interrupts

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add README with architecture overview, setup, and API docs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Implement notification system with server cluster, workers, emulators, and load testing

- Notification server: REST API, input validation, template rendering, Redis caching, RabbitMQ routing
- Notification worker: 4 dedicated queue consumers (APNS/FCM/SMS/EMAIL) with retry logic
- Third-party emulator: simulates delivery services with 95% success rate
- Device emulator: receives notifications, WebSocket broadcast, REST stats
- Load client: seeds test data, multi-threaded load test with p95/p99 reporting
- Infrastructure: PostgreSQL, Redis, RabbitMQ, Prometheus, Grafana with pre-provisioned dashboard
- Docker Compose with service health checks and replicas
- 27 unit tests covering validation, services, controllers, consumers
- Component diagram in drawio format

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Vercel deploy: use vercel pull to get env vars before build

Replaces shared-ci-workflows with inline steps so PUBLIC_NEON_AUTH_URL
and other Vercel env vars are available during the Astro build.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

start drawing immediately and share later via toolbar button.

Add three-condition pipeline with disease-aware adjacency correction

- Refactor two-mode pipeline (baseline/disease_specific) into three modes:
  random_atlas, disease_atlas, disease_atlas_rules (with backward-compat aliases)
- Add DISEASE_ANATOMY_RULES, NORMAL_ADJACENCY, DISEASE_ADJACENCY_OVERRIDES,
  and get_effective_adjacency() to config.py for TGA/DORV/ToF/HLHS/PuA rules
- Make anatomy_priors disease-aware: skip/override logic per active disease flag
- Add adjacency_correction.py: graph-based component relabelling using
  forbidden-pair detection, connectivity improvement scoring, and IoC with atlas
- Extend atlas.py select_for_case() with exclude_case_ids list param
- Add 28 new tests (12 adjacency + 6 disease-aware anatomy + 2 fragment + 8 pipeline)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Both use wave shape for smooth transition

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Theme all select/dropdown elements with bioluminescent styling

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Rebalance heuristic classifier for better Sonnet vs Opus delegation

Split complex verbs into standard-level (debug, review, implement) and
heavy-level (refactor, architect, restructure). Single verbs now score
in both tiers creating ambiguity — hybrid mode lets Haiku make the
final call on borderline cases. Heavy tier now requires multiple
stacked signals (multi-step + verb + length) for high confidence.

Results: tier distribution shifts from 8/3/4 to 8/5/2 (fast/std/heavy),
savings improve from 8% to 23% vs Opus baseline.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Render split-brain: auto-seed DB in entrypoint before Gunicorn starts

Gunicorn workers open SQLite connections at boot. If data is seeded
later via Shell, workers keep stale empty connections. Moving seed
and user creation into entrypoint.sh ensures data exists before
Gunicorn forks workers.

commit.message:
refactor: architecture cleanup, code quality fixes, expanded test suite

Architecture:
- split openDb() into private helpers: runMigrations, cleanupNfcPaths,
  restoreIgnorePatterns — each does one thing, easier to maintain
- fix cleanupNfcPaths: guard against vec_chunks not existing on first run
- add closeDb() for test isolation

Code quality:
- fix double getNoteMeta call in indexFile — single call, cached result
- fix NFD bug: mtime-only update now uses NFD-normalized path consistently
- improve indexFile return type: { error: string } instead of 'error',
  actual error message now propagates to reindex results
- batch resolveWikilinks: one SQL query instead of N — loads all notes
  into memory and resolves wikilinks via Map/Set lookups
- rename _indexQueue/_isIndexing to signal internal module state

Tests (30 total, was 25):
- checkModelChanged: same model returns false, changed model wipes DB
- NFD path storage: NFD paths stored/retrieved via links and BM25
- indexFile error message: non-existent file returns { error: string }

commit.message:
fix: rename lighthouserc.js to .cjs for ESM compat (#588)

Project has "type": "module" in package.json, so .js files are ESM.
lighthouserc uses module.exports (CommonJS). Renaming to .cjs fixes
the "module is not defined in ES module scope" error in CI.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix data loss: flush pending save to Firestore before logout/switch

Root cause: clearTimeout() cancelled the 800ms debounce timer before
it could write to Firestore. On logout localStorage is also cleared,
so data was permanently lost.

Fix: await saveToFirestoreNow() immediately before cancelling the timer
on logout or account switch, ensuring in-memory data is persisted first.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix mobile Game Day stuck on Loading… (fragment-root Teleport bug) (#3)

PR #1 added a second <Teleport to="#scene-overlay"> as a sibling root in
QuizPanel, creating a Fragment root.  When isMobile toggled in onMounted,
Vue's patchKeyedChildren tried to reconcile the fragment and crashed with
"Cannot set properties of null (reading '__vnode')", leaving quizState
permanently in its initial value and the play button frozen on "Loading…".

Root causes fixed:
- Remove the <Teleport to="#scene-overlay"> added in PR #1 (and its target
  <div id="scene-overlay"> from SceneView).  QuizPanel now has a single
  <Teleport to="#quiz-portal"> as its only root node.
- Keep <template><Teleport><div> on one unbroken line so no HTML comment
  vnode can become a Fragment sibling of the Teleport.
- Add defer prop (Vue 3.5) so the Teleport resolves #quiz-portal after the
  initial DOM flush, when the target (later in App.vue's tree) exists.
- Initialise isMobile synchronously — ref(window.innerWidth <= 600) — so
  the Teleport's disabled state is correct from the very first render,
  avoiding the disabled→enabled flip in onMounted that triggered
  patchKeyedChildren with a null anchor.
- Move the mobile streak/score bar inside the quiz panel (v-if="isMobile")
  rather than in a separate overlay Teleport.

Tests: adds tests/QuizPanel.test.js with 6 cases covering the idle-state
play button text/enabled state and the mobile Teleport DOM placement.

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Windows Unicode encoding; add sys.stdout.reconfigure utf-8

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Smart layout optimization — reduce scaling from 10 to 4 pages

Field-ops pages: map cells use aspect-ratio instead of fixed min-size
(was overflowing column), annotations compressed (5.5pt), workspace
cells 18→14px, tighter cipher margins, grid gap 10→6px.

Workout pages: tighter card padding/margins/gap, notes-box min-height
32→24px, session-header/story-prompt margins reduced. All 3-card
workout pages now fit without any scaling.

Worst-case scale improved from 0.775 to 0.965 (imperceptible).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(orchestration): enforce agent completion honesty and bulk scope verification (#60)

Agents were able to claim "all done" after processing only a subset of
files in a bulk task (e.g., updating 7 of 100+ markdown files). The
orchestrator had no mechanism to catch or challenge this claim, so it
was passed directly to the user as completion.

Three-part fix:

A. Agent system prompt — rules 8 & 9 added to MANDATORY AGENT CONDUCT RULES:
   - Rule 8 (COMPLETION HONESTY): agents must not claim a bulk task is
     complete without processing EVERY item. Must call list_dir first
     to know scope, report exact "X of Y files" count, and never say
     "done" while X < Y.
   - Rule 9 (BULK SCOPE DECLARATION): agents must declare scope before
     starting (N items) and confirm count after ("I processed N of N").
     If cycles ran out mid-task, they must report partial progress.

B. dispatchAgentAndAwait — new [SCOPE CHECK] block appended when:
   - Task contained bulk language (all / every / each / N files)
   - Agent's output claimed completion OR reported a count mismatch
   - Mismatch case: explicit "X of Y items" failure message + re-delegate
     instruction
   - Completion claim case: warning + required action (list_dir to verify)
   - Both cases: "NEVER relay this claim to the user without verification"

C. buildAgentSystemPrompt — VERIFICATION MANDATE injected for the
   orchestrator session only:
   - Check [FILE VERIFICATION] block before marking done
   - Check [SCOPE CHECK] block; if count mismatch, re-delegate
   - For any "all files" / "every X" task, run list_dir independently
   - Never relay an agent's "all done" claim without own verification
   - Auto re-delegate remaining items if verification fails

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(observability): reduce Logfire noise from page requests (#39)

* feat(observability): add external health check probe for Logfire

Adds a periodic external health probe that validates the full routing
chain (reverse proxy -> app) and reports results to Logfire. Feature-gated
with ENABLE_EXTERNAL_HEALTH_CHECK env var, probes APP_DOMAIN every 5min.
Tracks consecutive failures and warns after 3 in a row.

(/dashboard, /auth, /onboarding, /) were noise in the logs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Rewrite Apple Calendar module from AppleScript to JXA

AppleScript fundamentally cannot work with Calendar.app on modern macOS
because `event` is both a Calendar.app class and an AppleScript keyword.
JXA (JavaScript for Automation) has no such conflict and outputs JSON
natively, simplifying parsing. Also adds JXA language support to the
shared runAppleScript helper and fixes lint error in gmail-categories test.

commit.message:
fix: prevent H.264/H.265 codec strings from matching as TV episodes

The abbreviated TV episode pattern (e.g., .415. = S04E15) was matching
.264 in H.264 as S02E64. Added negative lookbehind for H/h/X/x.

fix: kick off collection sync immediately after Discogs OAuth callback

After OAuth completes, the user lands on /(tabs)/collection with an empty
crate because the collection screen's auto-sync useEffect has a timing
dependency on the tab being freshly mounted with discogsLinked=true.

Instead, fire sync(appUsername) directly in discogs-callback.tsx after
OAuth success — before navigating. Uses the useSyncCollection hook (not
raw syncService) so lastSyncTime is updated in the store on completion.

commit.message:
refactor: full codebase restructuring (Phase R) with 230 new tests

7-phase refactoring for maintainability without behavior changes:
- R1: Split MCP server.ts (1,871→~80 lines + 14 domain tool modules)
- R2: Extract 6 composables from +page.svelte (836→446 lines)
- R3: Split large components (TableCard/Sidebar/DomainModal/TableEditor)
- R4: Extract pure ops from erd.svelte.ts (922→530 lines, 6 ops modules)
- R5: Extract MobileNotice + StorageBanner components
- R6: Split 3 large test files into 14 focused files by dialect/category
- R7: Add api-helpers.ts (ok/err/notFound/unauthorized/forbidden)

New tests: table-ops(44), column-ops(41), fk-ops(47), memo-ops(34),
schema-ns-ops(36), domain-ops(15), api-helpers(13) = 230 total
73 test files, 2329 tests, svelte-check 0 errors

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Improve job observability with timeline metadata and log_to_job (#508)

@coderabbitai ignore

## Summary
- Add `metadata` field to `TimelineEvent` for structured output details
(brand codes, YouTube video IDs, Dropbox paths, GDrive file IDs)
- Add `log_to_job()` helper for API endpoints to write to Firestore job
log subcollections
- Plumb `timeline_metadata` through `job_manager.transition_to_state()`
and `firestore_service.update_job_status()`
- Capture output details in completion timeline events for full
traceability
- Log edit/delete cleanup operations to both Cloud Logging and Firestore
job logs
- Snapshot previous outputs before cleanup for audit trail
- Enhance distribution logging with video_id, folder path, and file IDs

## Context
After shipping "edit completed tracks," tracing edited jobs was
difficult because timeline events lacked output details, cleanup
operations weren't logged to Firestore, and previous outputs were
overwritten with no history. These changes make the job's Firestore
document self-documenting — any agent or human can read the timeline and
understand the full lifecycle.

## Test plan
- [x] 7 new unit tests for `TimelineEvent.metadata` and `log_to_job()`
helper
- [x] All 103 backend tests pass
- [ ] Verify in production after deploy: edit a test job and confirm
timeline metadata appears in Firestore

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---------

commit.message:
fix: align validate_run() with pipeline off-ramp architecture

The validator required every reviewer in config.reviewers_enabled to
be present in phase2, but the pipeline tolerates reviewer dropouts
when min_reviewers_required is met. This mismatch caused validation
to crash on degraded-but-successful runs (e.g. Claude fails Phase 2
while OpenAI + Gemini succeed).

Now validates only reviewers actually present in phase2 and enforces
the same min_reviewers_required quorum the pipeline uses.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Auto-retry missing answers individually during extraction

When answer extraction misses questions (common for written/open-ended answers),
automatically retry each missing answer individually on the relevant answer pages
using redoAnswerExtraction. Logs recovery results.
